## Types of HR certifications

HR certifications come in two broad forms, and understanding the difference helps you choose the right investment for where you are in your career.

Typically, an HR certification is a formal credential awarded by a professional body like SHRM or HRCI after passing a rigorous exam. These certifications validate your existing HR knowledge and are widely recognized by employers, particularly in compliance-focused or generalist HR roles. HRCI offers credentials ranging from entry-level (aPHR) to senior (SPHR), while SHRM’s competency-based certifications (SHRM-CP and SHRM-SCP) focus on situational judgment and practical application. For Canadian HR professionals, HRPA and CPHR serve a similar credentialing function.

An HR certificate program takes a different approach. Rather than validating what you already know, it focuses on building new, practical skills in a specific HR domain. AIHR’s certificate programs cover areas like AI in HR, HR business partnering, people analytics, and talent acquisition through hands-on, project-based learning. Completing an AIHR program also makes you eligible for SHRM PDCs and earns recertification credits with HRCI, HRPA, and CPHR, but it works just as well as a standalone investment, depending on your career goals and what you need most right now.

## HR certificate programs at AIHR

AIHR offers 16 certificate programs covering every major HR domain, from HR business partnering and people analytics to talent acquisition, compensation & benefits, and AI in HR. Each program is designed to build practical, job-ready skills through hands-on learning, real-world exercises, and a capstone project, so you finish with expertise you can put to work straight away.

Programs typically run between 30 and 40 hours and are fully self-paced, so you can work through them on your own schedule without putting your career on hold. As you complete each program, you earn a digital certificate to add to your professional profile and recertification credits toward leading HR credentials, like those from SHRM, HRCI, HRPA, and CPHR. The exact number of credits varies by program. You can find the full breakdown on each individual program page, or see all of them at a glance here.

With Full Academy Access membership, you get unlimited access to all 16 certificate programs alongside 85+ courses and mini courses, templates, resources, and more, giving you everything you need to build and maintain your HR expertise in one place.

## FAQ

Have more questions? Get in touch with one of our sales consultants.

### Which HR certification is most valuable?

The most valuable HR certification depends on your career goals. Formal credentials like SHRM-CP, SPHR, or PHR from HRCI are widely recognized by employers and often required for certain roles. But a credential alone won’t teach you the practical skills your organization needs from you right now.

AIHR’s certificate programs focus on building those skills, covering in-demand areas like people analytics, AI in HR, and HR business partnering through hands-on, applicable learning.

### Which HR certification is best for me?

It depends on where you are in your HR career and what you need most. If you want to validate your existing knowledge and earn a recognized credential, SHRM or HRCI certifications are strong options. If you want to build new skills in areas like people analytics, AI in HR, or HR business partnering, AIHR’s certificate programs are designed for exactly that. Many HR professionals pursue both over the course of their career.

### How long does it take to get an HR certification?

It varies by type. Preparing for a formal credential like the SHRM-CP or PHR typically takes several months of study on top of meeting eligibility requirements.

AIHR certificate programs run between 30 and 40 hours and are fully self-paced, so most professionals complete them within a few weeks to a few months, depending on their schedule.

### How to get an HR certificate online?

The most straightforward path is to enroll in an online HR certificate program. AIHR’s programs are fully online and self-paced, covering every major HR domain. You work through video lessons, real-world exercises, and a capstone project at your own pace, and receive a digital certificate upon completion.

AIHR’s digital certificates become an increasingly recognized signal of up-to-date expertise, and are a practical addition to your professional portfolio.

### What are the best online HR certificate programs?

The best online HR certificate programs combine practical, applicable content with recognized credentials and flexible learning. AIHR offers 16 certificate programs across domains like HR business partnering, people analytics, AI in HR, talent acquisition, and compensation & benefits, all designed around hands-on learning, eligible for SHRM PDCs, and recognized by major HR associations, including HRCI, HRPA, and CPHR.

### Is AIHR accredited?

AIHR is a registered education provider recognized by major HR associations worldwide. Completing an AIHR certificate program makes you eligible for SHRM PDCs and earns recertification credits with HRCI, HRPA, CPHR, and CIPD. AIHR is also a registered education institute on LinkedIn, so your digital certificates are directly verifiable on your professional profile.

### Is AIHR certification worth it?

For HR professionals who want to build practical, job-ready skills in a specific HR domain, AIHR’s certificate programs are a strong investment. The programs are hands-on and project-based, so you finish with skills you can apply straight away.

They also make you eligible for SHRM PDCs and earn recertification credits with major HR associations like HRCI, HRPA, and CPHR, adding further value if you already hold or are working toward a formal HR certification.
